title: Primary job selection
principles: The primary job must summarize the best-supported practical attack pattern rather than an isolated statistic, mechanic, or attack-family field.
Each job candidate must preserve its supporting evidence, relevant modes, conditions, restrictions, centrality, and confidence.
A candidate may be selected as primary only when positive evidence supports both its practical function and its dominance over competing candidates.
Capability, centrality, reliability, and evidence completeness must remain separate properties.
A highly conditional, incidental, or weakly supported capability must not become the primary job merely because no stronger label is available.
Secondary jobs must remain traceable to the modes and conditions that support them.
Use general_use for a genuinely broad or balanced practical profile, and use undetermined when the evidence is insufficient to identify a primary job.

sustained_damage: Repeated useful pressure across complete active and interruption cycles is the dominant supported function.
Sustained input, automatic repetition, or high fire rate alone is insufficient.
focused_damage: The attack pattern provides positive evidence of concentrating useful offensive value on one target or a narrowly defined target set.
Absence of multi-target behavior alone is insufficient.
group_clear: Applying useful damage to several targets is central and repeatedly supported by explicit multi-target mechanics.
Potential target reach without sufficient mechanical confidence is insufficient.
area_control: The attack pattern persistently, repeatedly, or conditionally influences a spatial region beyond a single damage event.
Radial damage and multi-target reach alone are insufficient.
status_application: Repeated eligible status opportunities and a favorable status relationship define the dominant practical pattern.
Status chance or instance count alone is insufficient.
precision_attacks: An explicit mechanical benefit or requirement tied to weak points, headshots, scoped hits, target locking, or another defined precision interaction dominates the attack pattern.
Accuracy, zoom, lock-on, or semi-automatic rhythm without a supported precision benefit is insufficient.
heavy_attacks: Heavy attacks are comparatively better supported than other melee attack families and define the practical use pattern.
Generic heavy-attack fields, high heavy damage, or wind-up alone are insufficient.
general_use: Several practical functions have comparable support, or the attack pattern is intentionally broad without one clearly dominant specialization.
This classification does not imply equal effectiveness across all jobs.
undetermined: The available candidate evidence is incomplete, conflicting, or insufficient to establish a dominant practical function.
Do not substitute general_use for missing evidence.

multiple_relevant_modes: Use an explicit whole-weapon relevance criterion before selecting one mode's job as the weapon-level primary job.
Preserve materially different functions from other relevant modes as secondary job records.
balanced_job_candidates: Use general_use only when several candidates have comparable positive evidence and together form a broad practical profile.
Use undetermined instead when the apparent balance results from incomplete evidence.
conditional_job_candidate: Preserve the candidate's activation conditions and restrictions.
Reduce its dominance or retain it as secondary when the condition is not representative of normal operation.
low_confidence_candidate: Do not select the candidate as primary solely from high theoretical potential.
Preserve uncertainty when activation, target reach, component eligibility, or practical repetition is insufficiently defined.
shared_primary_evidence: When two candidates depend on the same evidence but describe different semantic dimensions, select the one that best represents the declared analysis taxonomy.
Do not combine them into an unsupported hybrid label.

exceptions: Do not select sustained_damage from sustained rhythm, automatic input, beam delivery, or high fire rate alone.
Do not select focused_damage solely from the absence of multi-target evidence.
Do not select group_clear from an aoe label, radius, chaining flag, Punch Through value, pellet count, or theoretical target limit alone.
Do not select area_control from radial damage, persistent visuals, or multi-target capability alone.
Do not select status_application from status chance alone.
Do not select precision_attacks from accuracy, zoom, projectile delivery, lock-on, or semi-automatic behavior without an explicit precision relationship.
Do not select heavy_attacks merely because heavy-attack records exist.
Do not merge evidence or jobs across modes without an explicit whole-weapon relevance criterion.
Do not treat the primary job as a statement of weapon power, quality, or optimality.
